heading: Prohibition on sale of certain products containing specified phthalates
label: 2057c Prohibition on sale of certain products containing specified phthalates

children.heading: Prohibition on the sale of certain products containing phthalates
children.label: a Prohibition on the sale of certain products containing phthalates
children.text: Beginning on the date that is 180 days after , it shall be unlawful for any person to manufacture for sale, offer for sale, distribute in commerce, or import into the United States any children’s toy or child care article that contains concentrations of more than 0.1 percent of di-(2-ethylhexyl) phthalate (DEHP), dibutyl phthalate (DBP), or benzyl butyl phthalate (BBP).August 14, 2008

children.heading: Prohibition on the sale of additional products containing certain phthalates
children.label: b Prohibition on the sale of additional products containing certain phthalates

children.heading: Interim prohibition
children.label: 1 Interim prohibition
children.text: Beginning on the date that is 180 days after , and until a final rule is promulgated under paragraph (3), it shall be unlawful for any person to manufacture for sale, offer for sale, distribute in commerce, or import into the United States any children’s toy that can be placed in a child’s mouth or child care article that contains concentrations of more than 0.1 percent of diisononyl phthalate (DINP), diisodecyl phthalate (DIDP), or di-n-octyl phthalate (DnOP).August 14, 2008

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: A Appointment
children.text: Not earlier than 180 days after , the Commission shall begin the process of appointing a Chronic Hazard Advisory Panel pursuant to the procedures of section 28 of the Consumer Product Safety Act () to study the effects on children’s health of all phthalates and phthalate alternatives as used in children’s toys and child care articles.August 14, 200815 U.S.C. 2077

children.label: i
children.text: examine all of the potential health effects (including endocrine disrupting effects) of the full range of phthalates;

children.label: ii
children.text: consider the potential health effects of each of these phthalates both in isolation and in combination with other phthalates;

children.label: iii
children.text: examine the likely levels of children’s, pregnant women’s, and others’ exposure to phthalates, based on a reasonable estimation of normal and foreseeable use and abuse of such products;

children.label: iv
children.text: consider the cumulative effect of total exposure to phthalates, both from children’s products and from other sources, such as personal care products;

children.label: v
children.text: review all relevant data, including the most recent, best-available, peer-reviewed, scientific studies of these phthalates and phthalate alternatives that employ objective data collection practices or employ other objective methods;

children.label: vi
children.text: consider the health effects of phthalates not only from ingestion but also as a result of dermal, hand-to-mouth, or other exposure;

children.label: vii
children.text: consider the level at which there is a reasonable certainty of no harm to children, pregnant women, or other susceptible individuals and their offspring, considering the best available science, and using sufficient safety factors to account for uncertainties regarding exposure and susceptibility of children, pregnant women, and other potentially susceptible individuals; and

children.label: viii
children.text: consider possible similar health effects of phthalate alternatives used in children’s toys and child care articles.

children.heading: Report
children.label: C Report
children.text: Not later than 180 days after completing its examination, the panel appointed under subparagraph (A) shall report to the Commission the results of the examination conducted under this section and shall make recommendations to the Commission regarding any phthalates (or combinations of phthalates) in addition to those identified in subsection (a) or phthalate alternatives that the panel determines should be declared banned hazardous substances.

children.label: A
children.text: determine, based on such report, whether to continue in effect the prohibition under paragraph (1), in order to ensure a reasonable certainty of no harm to children, pregnant women, or other susceptible individuals with an adequate margin of safety; and

children.label: B
children.text: evaluate the findings and recommendations of the Chronic Hazard Advisory Panel and declare any children’s product containing any phthalates to be a banned hazardous product under section 8 of the Consumer Product Safety Act (), as the Commission determines necessary to protect the health of children.15 U.S.C. 2057

children.heading: Application
children.label: c Application
children.text: Effective on , subsections (a) and (b)(1) and any rule promulgated under subsection (b)(3) shall apply to any plasticized component part of a children’s toy or child care article or any other component part of a children’s toy or child care article that is made of other materials that may contain phthalates.August 12, 20111See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Exclusion for inaccessible component parts
children.label: d Exclusion for inaccessible component parts

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The prohibitions established under subsections (a) and (b) shall not apply to any component part of a children’s toy or child care article that is not accessible to a child through normal and reasonably foreseeable use and abuse of such product, as determined by the Commission. A component part is not accessible under this paragraph if such component part is not physically exposed by reason of a sealed covering or casing and does not become physically exposed through reasonably foreseeable use and abuse of the product. Reasonably foreseeable use and abuse shall include swallowing, mouthing, breaking, or other children’s activities, and the aging of the product.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: The Commission may revoke an exclusion or all exclusions granted under paragraph (1) at any time and require that any or all component parts manufactured after such exclusion is revoked comply with the prohibitions established under subsections (a) and (b) if the Commission finds, based on scientific evidence, that such compliance is necessary to protect the public health or safety.

children.label: A
children.text: promulgate a rule providing guidance with respect to what product components, or classes of components, will be considered to be inaccessible for purposes of paragraph (1); or

children.label: B
children.text: adopt the same guidance with respect to inaccessibility that was adopted by the Commission with regards to accessibility of lead under , with additional consideration, as appropriate, of whether such component can be placed in a child’s mouth.section 1278a(b)(2)(B) of this title

children.heading: Application pending commission guidance
children.label: 4 Application pending commission guidance
children.text: Until the Commission promulgates a rule pursuant to paragraph (3), the determination of whether a product component is inaccessible to a child shall be made in accordance with the requirements laid out in paragraph (1) for considering a component to be inaccessible to a child.

children.heading: Treatment of violation
children.label: e Treatment of violation
children.text: A violation of subsection (a) or (b)(1) or any rule promulgated by the Commission under subsection (b)(3) shall be treated as a violation of section 19(a)(1) of the Consumer Product Safety Act ().15 U.S.C. 2068(a)(1)

children.heading: Treatment as consumer product safety standards; effect on State laws
children.label: f Treatment as consumer product safety standards; effect on State laws
children.text: Subsections (a) and (b)(1) and any rule promulgated under subsection (b)(3) shall be considered consumer product safety standards under the Consumer Product Safety Act [ et seq.]. Nothing in this section or the Consumer Product Safety Act ( et seq.) shall be construed to preempt or otherwise affect any State requirement with respect to any phthalate alternative not specifically regulated in a consumer product safety standard under the Consumer Product Safety Act.15 U.S.C. 205115 U.S.C. 2051

children.label: A
children.text: The term “phthalate alternative” means any common substitute to a phthalate, alternative material to a phthalate, or alternative plasticizer.

children.label: B
children.text: The term “children’s toy” means a consumer product designed or intended by the manufacturer for a child 12 years of age or younger for use by the child when the child plays.

children.label: C
children.text: The term “child care article” means a consumer product designed or intended by the manufacturer to facilitate sleep or the feeding of children age 3 and younger, or to help such children with sucking or teething.

children.label: D
children.text: The term “consumer product” has the meaning given such term in section 3(a)(1) of the Consumer Product Safety Act ().15 U.S.C. 2052(a)(1)

children.label: i
children.text: A statement by a manufacturer about the intended use of such product, including a label on such product if such statement is reasonable.

children.label: ii
children.text: Whether the product is represented in its packaging, display, promotion, or advertising as appropriate for use by children of the ages specified.

children.label: iii
children.text: Whether the product is commonly recognized by consumers as being intended for use by a child of the ages specified.

children.label: iv
children.text: The Age Determination guidelines issued by the Commission staff in September 2002 and any successor to such guidelines.

children.heading: Toy that can be placed in a child’s mouth
children.label: B Toy that can be placed in a child’s mouth
children.text: For purposes of this section a toy can be placed in a child’s mouth if any part of the toy can actually be brought to the mouth and kept in the mouth by a child so that it can be sucked and chewed. If the children’s product can only be licked, it is not regarded as able to be placed in the mouth. If a toy or part of a toy in one dimension is smaller than 5 centimeters, it can be placed in the mouth.
